display.purpose: View headers and the largest panel titles. Playfair Display, the editorial voice.

title.purpose: Panel and modal headings (e.g. 'Session Log'). Playfair at small size.

body.purpose: Default reading text and descriptions. IBM Plex Mono, capped at 65-75ch in prose.

data.purpose: The dense workhorse: session rows, metadata, message bodies, timestamps.

label.purpose: Tracked uppercase section eyebrows and column headers. <=4 words.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Ember-tinted focus ring on inputs and interactive elements. The standard focus treatment.

shadows.name: ember-glow
shadows.purpose: Liveness emphasis: connection pulse, active control. Never decorative.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Slide-in detail and session-log panels that genuinely float above the board.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Dialogs lifted clear of the backdrop.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transition for hovers and color shifts.

motion.name: ease-deliberate
motion.purpose: Deliberate state changes (drag, expand) wanting a touch more presence.

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: The primary action affordance in the app shell. Quiet at rest, lifts on hover. No filled CTA buttons.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-btn" aria-label="History"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="9"/><path d="M12 7v5l3 2"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 6px 8px; background: var(--bg-deep, #101114); color: var(--text-muted, #7d808a); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-icon-btn:hover { background: var(--bg-hover, #282a30); color: var(--text-primary, #f0f1f3); border-color: var(--border, #363840); } .ds-icon-btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent-dim, rgba(232,111,51,0.22)); }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Filter / count pill. Neutral at rest; active state tints toward the ember as a whisper, never a flood.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 2px 8px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-secondary, #c2c4c9); background: var(--bg-deep, #101114); border: 1px solid var(--border, #363840); border-radius: 4px; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--text-secondary, #c2c4c9) 30%, var(--border, #363840)); } .ds-chip--active { color: color-mix(in srgb, #e86f33 80%, var(--text-secondary, #c2c4c9)); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, #e86f33 50%, var(--border, #363840)); background: color-mix(in srgb, #e86f33 9%, var(--bg-deep, #101114)); }

components.name: Task Card
components.description: Session/task card. Flat at rest on the surface tone; selection shown by the ember focus ring, not a shadow.
components.html:
Improve CCK sidebar visual design and UI/UX

main
components.css: .ds-card { padding: 12px; background: var(--bg-surface, #16181c); border: 1px solid var(--border, #363840); border-radius: 6px; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--text-secondary, #c2c4c9) 30%, var(--border, #363840)); } .ds-card[aria-selected="true"] { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent-dim, rgba(232,111,51,0.22)); } .ds-card__title { font-family: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text-primary, #f0f1f3); margin: 0; } .ds-card__meta { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted, #7d808a); }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Inset well cut into the surface. Ember focus ring; border does not jump to full accent.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-primary, #f0f1f3); background: var(--bg-deep, #101114); border: 1px solid var(--border, #363840); border-radius: 6px; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-muted, #7d808a); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent-dim, rgba(232,111,51,0.22)); }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: Tracked uppercase mono eyebrow used for sidebar sections and kanban column headers.
components.css: .ds-label { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-muted, #7d808a); }

narrative.overview: Claude Code Hub is the instrument a developer keeps open while many Claude Code sessions, agents, and tasks run at once. It reads like a working notebook, not an app: editorial serif section-heads (Playfair Display) sit directly above dense monospace data (IBM Plex Mono), a print-style contrast axis that signals 'this is a record, kept precisely.' The field is quiet and cool-neutral; a single terracotta ember marks what is live. The system ships in two sibling skins: a near-black instrument (default) and a warm-paper notebook (light), sharing one ember accent, one type pairing, and one tonal-first depth model. It rejects the bright SaaS-dashboard look, glaring white surfaces, and anything childish.
keyCharacteristics: Editorial serif headings over monospace data; no third typeface.
Dark-first, with a warm-paper light theme as an equal sibling.
One terracotta ember as the only non-functional saturated color.
Tonal-first depth: surfaces lift by lightness step, not by shadow.
Dense by design; whitespace spent for clarity, not decoration.

rules.name: The One Ember Rule
rules.body: The terracotta accent is the only saturated color allowed for non-functional use, and it stays under 10% of any screen. Status hues appear only bound to the data they describe, never as decoration.

rules.name: The No-Pure-White Rule
rules.body: In the light theme, no surface is #ffffff. The brightest allowed value is the warm elevated off-white #fbfaf9.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Serif speaks for structure (headings, titles); mono speaks for content and data. A heading in mono or a number in serif is a bug. Never introduce a third typeface.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: A resting card, row, or panel has no drop shadow. Shadows appear only for focus (ring), liveness (glow), or genuine float (panels, modals).

dos: Do convey depth with the tonal ramp first (field -> surface -> elevated -> hover); reach for a border only when tone is not enough.
Do keep the ember under 10% of any screen, and bind every status hue to the data it describes.
Do keep headings in Playfair and all data in IBM Plex Mono; weight and the serif/mono axis carry hierarchy.
Do soften light-theme dividers (color-mix(... var(--border) 35%, transparent)) so panels separate without hard lines.
Do show focus with the ember ring (0 0 0 2px accent-dim) and reserve glow/shadow for liveness and true floating.
donts: Don't use any #ffffff surface in the light theme; the warm elevated off-white #fbfaf9 is the ceiling.
Don't box every region in a high-contrast border or drop an ambient shadow on a resting card.
Don't ship the bright SaaS-dashboard look: hero-metric tiles, identical icon-heading-text card grids, gradient accents.
Don't go childish: bubbly radii, bright primary palettes, emoji-as-UI, gamified flourishes.
Don't add a third typeface, set body copy in ALL CAPS, or put a number in the serif.
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right greater than 1px as an accent stripe on new components.
